concept.title: Typical Problems in AI-Generated DITA
concept.shortdesc: Identify recurring structural defects in generated DITA to prioritize deterministic refactoring actions.

ul.li: Multiple candidate entry maps with no clear main map.
Flat map hierarchies with weak section boundaries.
Map references that duplicate or conflict with topic references.
Topic titles and navtitles that do not align.
Mixed concept, task, and reference semantics in a single topic.
Generated topics with weak metadata and missing role signals.
Broken href paths after package reorganization.
Media files copied into unexpected folders.
External references that should be internalized for delivery builds.

section.title: How to prioritize fixes
section.p: Start with issues that block deterministic planning and execution: missing entry map certainty, broken references, and repeated duplicate topics. Then address structure quality improvements that improve navigation and reuse.
